relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After Sabbath, the women bring spices to anoint the body.
At sunrise on the first day, they come to the tomb.
Their question is practical: who will move the stone?
Looking up, they find the great stone already rolled away.
Inside the tomb, a young man in white alarms them.
Do not be alarmed: the crucified Nazarene has been raised and is not here.
The message sends them to disciples and Peter, toward Galilee and promised seeing.
Trembling, astonishment, silence, and fear hold the open-ended ending.

textual_notes.mystical_note: The longer ending is a later witness to resurrection proclamation and mission tradition. It is not used as the primary restored ending here.
The shorter ending is also treated as later textual tradition. Mark’s primary ending remains stark, trembling, and open.
